item.title: Global Long Bond Yields Climb to Highest in Almost Two Decades
item.description: The selloff in longer-maturity government bonds has pushed up yields to levels last seen during the global financial crisis, and strategists are warning the losses have room to run.

item.title: Prabowo to Tighten Control of Indonesia’s Commodity Exports
item.description: Indonesian President Prabowo Subianto laid out plans to tighten state control of commodity exports as the government contends with mounting fiscal pressures and a plunging rupiah.

item.title: Macron’s Central-Bank Pick Moulin Faces Parliamentary Showdown
item.description: Emmanuel Macron’s pick to lead the Bank of France will be grilled on Wednesday by lawmakers who could scupper the president’s plan to install a top ally at the helm of monetary policy.

item.title: Sri Lankan Rupee Weakness Intensifying Due to Speculation, Official Says
item.description: A slide in the Sri Lankan rupee, Asia’s worst performing currency this month, has intensified due to speculation of further weakening, according to Deputy Minister of Finance Anil Jayantha Fernando.

item.title: China Seeks to Extend US Trade Truce, Sets Limit on 301 Tariffs
item.description: China said it will continue negotiating with the US to extend a trade truce reached last October, as ties between the world’s two largest economies further stabilize.

item.title: Bond Market Fright May End Cheap Funds Era
item.description: What’s the same, and different, between now and the run-up to the Global Financial Crisis.

item.title: Australia’s Core Lithium Restarts Mining After Prices Rebound
item.description: Core Lithium Ltd. has restarted mining at its Finniss project in Australia’s Northern Territory, as a rebound in prices of the battery material prompts some companies to resume mothballed operations.

item.title: Wheat Drops on Lack of Chinese Confirmation of Grain Purchases
item.description: US wheat futures led grains lower as sentiment soured due to a lack of confirmation from China on the Trump administration’s announcement of billions of dollars in agricultural purchases.

item.title: Africa’s Biggest Economy Set to Hike Interest Rates on War Shock as Peers Hold
item.description: South Africa is poised to join a handful of central banks in raising interest rates to contain the inflationary fallout from the Iran war in coming weeks, even as most peers keep borrowing costs on hold.

item.title: Industrial Metals Slide as Inflation Fears Fuel Bearish Mood
item.description: Copper edged lower and iron ore declined for a fifth day as concerns mount that global central banks will take a hawkish turn to fight inflation stemming from the war in the Middle East.

item.title: China Bonds Buck Global Rout With Yields Hitting Nine-Month Low
item.description: China’s bonds are diverging further from peers as a fragile economic recovery and ample market liquidity keep local yields anchored despite a global debt selloff.

item.title: Alibaba Unveils New AI Chip for Training and Inferencing
item.description: Alibaba Group Holding Ltd. added a new processor to its expanding AI technology stack, enhancing a push to cover every aspect of artificial intelligence development.

item.title: Japan’s Longer Bonds Gain After Auction Brings Short-Term Relief
item.description: Japan’s longer-maturity government bonds gained after a 20-year auction reflected firm investor interest.

item.title: Hong Kong Targets July Launch for New Gold-Clearing System
item.description: Hong Kong plans to launch a new gold-clearing system by July, advancing the city’s ambitions to become a global hub for bullion trading.

item.title: Pimco Favors Japan’s 30-Year Bonds, Says Yield Curve ‘Too Steep’
item.description: Pacific Investment Management Co. is seeing an opportunity in Japan’s 30-year sovereign bonds at a time when concerns over inflation and government spending have pushed those yields to record highs.

item.title: OpenAI Commits $234 Million for New AI Lab in Singapore
item.description: OpenAI Inc. is setting up an AI lab in Singapore through a multiyear partnership with the local government, part of a broader effort to support the city-state’s artificial intelligence ambitions.

item.title: BofA Sees Three Catalysts That Could Turn It Bullish on Yen
item.description: BofA Securities is turning less bearish on the yen and sees three catalysts that could eventually turn it outright bullish, even as the currency drifts back toward 160 per dollar.

item.title: Japan’s Super-Long Bonds See First Foreign Outflow Since 2024
item.description: Overseas investors sold more Japan’s super-long government bonds than they bought for the first time in well over a year as the market grappled with concerns about inflation and increased fiscal spending.

item.title: Korea Exchange Is Said to Launch Weekly Options on Single Stocks
item.description: Investors will soon have another way to play the wild gyrations in South Korea’s stock market.

item.title: Samsung Faces Risk of Chip Disruption After Labor Talks Collapse
item.description: Talks between Samsung Electronics Co. and its largest labor union broke down, with the union telling local media it will go ahead with a work stoppage as planned, which looks set to disrupt operations at the world’s largest memory chipmaker.

item.title: Foreign Flows Into Indian Stocks Show Signs of Recovery
item.description: Nifty has fared better than Asia’s stock benchmark in recent sessions.

item.title: Indonesian Markets Rebound Ahead of Prabowo’s Parliament Speech
item.description: Indonesia’s currency and stocks rebounded Wednesday in volatile trade, erasing earlier losses on optimism ahead of a key budget speech by President Prabowo Subianto.

item.title: AustralianSuper Hires AI Chief as Pension Funds Race to Adapt
item.description: AustralianSuper, the nation’s largest pension fund, has hired its first head of artificial intelligence as the industry grapples with how rapidly evolving technology could reshape retirement finance.

item.title: Aussie Faces Headwinds as Global Bond Selloff Narrows Yield Edge
item.description: The Australian dollar is coming under renewed pressure as the yield advantage it holds over US assets wanes and investors brace for a possible escalation to hostilities in the Middle East.

item.title: World Underestimating Iran War Impact on LNG, Says Woodside
item.description: The world is underestimating how long the impact of the Iran war will last on global supply of liquefied natural gas, according to Australia’s top exporter of the fuel.

item.title: Palm Oil and Coal Stocks Drop on Indonesian Export Control Plan
item.description: Indonesian palm oil and coal stocks tumbled on the prospect of tighter state control over commodity exports from Southeast Asia’s largest economy.

item.title: Global Banks Fill Void in Australian AT1 Market After Phaseout
item.description: The decision by Australia’s banking watchdog to phase out Additional Tier 1 bonds marked the end of local lenders issuing the riskiest type of bank debt. Big international banks are now moving in to fill the void.

item.title: Morgan Stanley Japan Head Says BOJ Hike Key to Strengthening Yen
item.description: Morgan Stanley’s Japan chief said he is hoping that the yen strengthens to around 140 against the dollar, and that Bank of Japan action is key for that to happen.

item.title: Here’s a Look at India’s Measures to Stem Hit From Oil Shock
item.description: India is rushing to contain the fallout of the Iran war, as soaring oil prices hammer the rupee and drive record foreign outflows from local stocks.

item.title: Tencent-Backer IDG Capital Sets Sights on $2 Billion Growth Fund
item.description: IDG Capital, an investment firm that has backed Tencent Holdings Ltd. and Coinbase Global Inc., is seeking to raise about $2 billion for a growth fund, according to people familiar with the matter.
